2. Automated Budget Planning & Tracking Process 
 The current process of Budget Planning relies on the circulation of 
Excel worksheets across the 25 Departments of IIROSA to be filled 
with forecasts for the next fiscal year.  IIROSA currently has chart of accounts comprised of ten (10) 
segments.  It is preferable to maintain the Excel worksheet interface & 
Hyperion forms for the department users to minimize training 
and initial rollout efforts. 3. Document-Based workflows through Content Management System 
 IIROSA currently uses a content management system that needs 
to be replaced due to limitations in its capabilities and difficulty in 
maintaining it.  Unstructured data from the old Content Management System 
should be migrated to the new system. 4. Geographical dashboard view of IIROSA projects.  A separate world map dashboard should be made available to 
IIROSA top management users, by which they can visualize the 
status of progress on the various projects constructed around the 
world.  The map should be automatically populated with real-time 
information supplied by IIROSA staff at different stages of those 
projects.  Project information should include, but not limited to: Project 
Definition details, Project Progress details, Project Photos folder, 
Project Financial Statements and other project related information. 5. Performance Scorecard Dashboard

IIROSA e-Relief RFP 
 
 
 
Confidential Page 7 of 18 
 
 IIROSA intends to measure its operational performance using 
state of the art technology, based on management best practices.  Need To Implement 6 Sigma Methods in all IIROSA Processes. 3.3 NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS 
 
 Implementation Approach 
 The project execution should be based on the principles and 
practices of Enterprise Architecture (EA). All EA layers should be 
considered: Strategy, Business, Data, Applications and Technology; 
 The bidder should develop the detailed EA linking IIROSA’s business 
strategy down to the relevant technology components across all IIROSA 
departments and branches.  The bidder should propose the following: 
 EA Principles 
 EA Framework 
 EA Lifecycle 
 EA Governance Model 
 EA Tool 
 The successful bidder will be required to record all development 
activities through the use of CAMTASIA desktop recording tool.  Emergency Relief services by definition need to be agile, to quickly 
respond to the urgent needs that surface with every new crisis.  New eRelief services should follow the Service Oriented 
Architecture style.  eRelief process flows should be analyzed from a service 
oriented approach, breaking them up into smaller re-usable 
service components.  Technical Database Tier Upgrade 
 Technical upgrade of Oracle 10g database to 11g technology.  Implementation of 
 Partitioning 
 Real Application Clusters 
 Diagnostics Pack 
 Tuning Pack 
 Configuration Management Pack for Oracle Database 
 Change Management Pack 
 Provisioning and Patch Automation Pack for Database

IIROSA e-Relief RFP 
 
 
 
Confidential Page 8 of 18 
 
 Database Vault 
 Oracle Active Data Guard 
 Reporting 
 Existing Legacy Systems have reports developed in Oracle 6i 
Reports, which have to be migrated to the new platform.  All Reporting must be implemented using the BI Reporting 
engine.  All historical data to be entered into the data warehouse for 
reporting purposes.  The system should meet W3C Accessibility Standards; 
 The system should be secure; 
 The system should be reliable and scalable.  The system should meet IIROSA technical standards outlined in the 
Appendix A. 
3.4 PROJECT DELIVERABLES 
The successful bidder will provide IIROSA with the following, including but 
not limited to: 
 
A. Enterprise Architecture repository, based on an industry leading EA 
tool. The EA repository will contain the collection of all the relevant 
information from the different levels (Strategy, Business, Data, 
Application and Technology) of the organization. B. Project Implementation Plan The successful bidder will deliver a comp rehensive Project Management 
Plan that includes a Project Charter, Project Communications Plan, 
Project Schedule and Risk & Issue Register. C. System Detailed Software Requirements Specification 
The successful bidder will deliver the completed business requi rements 
together with a detailed software requirements specification document. D. System Logical Design 
The successful Bidder will develop and deliver the logical design of the 
proposed system. This will include Data Flow Diagrams (DFD) and other 
logical designs which can help the development of a robust system. E. System Physical Design 
Building on the logical design, the successful bidder will develop and 
submit the physical design of eRelief. This will include Data Flow 
Diagrams (DFD), Entity Relationship Diagrams (ERD) and the user -
interface and screen layout. IIROSA e-Relief RFP 
 
 
 
Confidential Page 9 of 18 
 
 
F. System Development The successful bidder will develop the application in compliance with 
IIROSA infrastructure and standards outlined in the Appendix A. This 
will also include integration with any required external system. G. Implementation 
The successful Bidder will implement the system after successfully 
testing on IIROSA QA environment (staging environment). If required, 
the system will also be tested at other departments‟ locations. H. Handover to IIROSA 
The successful bidder will hand over all the documentation to IIROSA 
including application source code, which will remain the property of 
IIROSA. At the time of handover the Bidder will ensure to provide 
technical support for One Year. I. eRelief User Guides 
The successful bidder will develop a bilingual User Manual for the users 
of eRelief application to use. The same user manual should be accessible 
online, and eRelief application should also provide context-sensitive help 
wherever needed for usability and user-friendliness purposes. J. Post Handover 
The successful bidder will use previously agreed KPIs to measure the 
satisfaction and success factors of the project.

2. Automated Budget Planning & Tracking Process 
 The current process of Budget Planning relies on the circulation of 
Excel worksheets across the 25 Departments of IIROSA to be filled 
with forecasts for the next fiscal year.  IIROSA currently has chart of accounts comprised of ten (10) 
segments.  It is preferable to maintain the Excel worksheet interface & 
Hyperion forms for the department users to minimize training 
and initial rollout efforts. 3. Document-Based workflows through Content Management System 
 IIROSA currently uses a content management system that needs 
to be replaced due to limitations in its capabilities and difficulty in 
maintaining it.  Unstructured data from the old Content Management System 
should be migrated to the new system. 4. Geographical dashboard view of IIROSA projects.  A separate world map dashboard should be made available to 
IIROSA top management users, by which they can visualize the 
status of progress on the various projects constructed around the 
world.  The map should be automatically populated with real-time 
information supplied by IIROSA staff at different stages of those 
projects.  Project information should include, but not limited to: Project 
Definition details, Project Progress details, Project Photos folder, 
Project Financial Statements and other project related information. 5. Performance Scorecard Dashboard

IIROSA e-Relief RFP 
 
 
 
Confidential Page 7 of 18 
 
 IIROSA intends to measure its operational performance using 
state of the art technology, based on management best practices.  Need To Implement 6 Sigma Methods in all IIROSA Processes. 3.3 NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS 
 
 Implementation Approach 
 The project execution should be based on the principles and 
practices of Enterprise Architecture (EA). All EA layers should be 
considered: Strategy, Business, Data, Applications and Technology; 
 The bidder should develop the detailed EA linking IIROSA’s business 
strategy down to the relevant technology components across all IIROSA 
departments and branches.  The bidder should propose the following: 
 EA Principles 
 EA Framework 
 EA Lifecycle 
 EA Governance Model 
 EA Tool 
 The successful bidder will be required to record all development 
activities through the use of CAMTASIA desktop recording tool.  Emergency Relief services by definition need to be agile, to quickly 
respond to the urgent needs that surface with every new crisis.  New eRelief services should follow the Service Oriented 
Architecture style.  eRelief process flows should be analyzed from a service 
oriented approach, breaking them up into smaller re-usable 
service components.  Technical Database Tier Upgrade 
 Technical upgrade of Oracle 10g database to 11g technology.  Implementation of 
 Partitioning 
 Real Application Clusters 
 Diagnostics Pack 
 Tuning Pack 
 Configuration Management Pack for Oracle Database 
 Change Management Pack 
 Provisioning and Patch Automation Pack for Database

IIROSA e-Relief RFP 
 
 
 
Confidential Page 8 of 18 
 
 Database Vault 
 Oracle Active Data Guard 
 Reporting 
 Existing Legacy Systems have reports developed in Oracle 6i 
Reports, which have to be migrated to the new platform.  All Reporting must be implemented using the BI Reporting 
engine.  All historical data to be entered into the data warehouse for 
reporting purposes.  The system should meet W3C Accessibility Standards; 
 The system should be secure; 
 The system should be reliable and scalable.  The system should meet IIROSA technical standards outlined in the 
Appendix A. 
3.4 PROJECT DELIVERABLES 
The successful bidder will provide IIROSA with the following, including but 
not limited to: 
 
A. Enterprise Architecture repository, based on an industry leading EA 
tool. The EA repository will contain the collection of all the relevant 
information from the different levels (Strategy, Business, Data, 
Application and Technology) of the organization. B. Project Implementation Plan The successful bidder will deliver a comp rehensive Project Management 
Plan that includes a Project Charter, Project Communications Plan, 
Project Schedule and Risk & Issue Register. C. System Detailed Software Requirements Specification 
The successful bidder will deliver the completed business requi rements 
together with a detailed software requirements specification document. D. System Logical Design 
The successful Bidder will develop and deliver the logical design of the 
proposed system. This will include Data Flow Diagrams (DFD) and other 
logical designs which can help the development of a robust system. E. System Physical Design 
Building on the logical design, the successful bidder will develop and 
submit the physical design of eRelief. This will include Data Flow 
Diagrams (DFD), Entity Relationship Diagrams (ERD) and the user -
interface and screen layout. IIROSA e-Relief RFP 
 
 
 
Confidential Page 9 of 18 
 
 
F. System Development The successful bidder will develop the application in compliance with 
IIROSA infrastructure and standards outlined in the Appendix A. This 
will also include integration with any required external system. G. Implementation 
The successful Bidder will implement the system after successfully 
testing on IIROSA QA environment (staging environment). If required, 
the system will also be tested at other departments‟ locations. H. Handover to IIROSA 
The successful bidder will hand over all the documentation to IIROSA 
including application source code, which will remain the property of 
IIROSA. At the time of handover the Bidder will ensure to provide 
technical support for One Year. I. eRelief User Guides 
The successful bidder will develop a bilingual User Manual for the users 
of eRelief application to use. The same user manual should be accessible 
online, and eRelief application should also provide context-sensitive help 
wherever needed for usability and user-friendliness purposes. J. Post Handover 
The successful bidder will use previously agreed KPIs to measure the 
satisfaction and success factors of the project.
output: The chapter outlines that the successful bidder must follow Enterprise Architecture principles and implement various Oracle technologies and tools, upgrade legacy systems, and ensure process improvements. It specifies functional requirements like integrated relief services across multiple programs, automated budget planning, document workflows, geographical dashboards, and performance scorecards. Non-functional requirements include adherence to EA layers, agile emergency relief services, database upgrades, reporting standards, security, scalability, and compliance with IIROSA technical standards.

output: The successful bidder must deliver an Enterprise Architecture repository, a comprehensive project implementation plan, detailed software requirements, logical and physical system designs, and develop the application according to IIROSA standards. They must implement and test the system, hand over all documentation and source code with one year of technical support, provide bilingual user guides with online help, and measure project success using agreed KPIs after handover.

instruction: In RFP for eRelief, quote the section that explains the detailed requirements for proposal submission including the background, approach, project plan, references, financial offer, and proposal quality.
input:
DOCUMENT_TAG: RFP for eRelief
SECTION_LEVEL: chapter
SECTION_INDEX: 2
SECTION_TITLE: Proposal Submission Procedures and Requirements
SECTION_DESCRIPTION: Instructions and guidelines for submitting proposals, including required content, format, and quality expectations.
SECTION_TEXT:
4 PROPOSAL PROCEDURES AND INSTRUCTIONS 
4.1 SUBMISSION OF THE PROPOSALS 
All the proposals must include the following: 
 
A. BACKGROUND, QUALIFICATIONS AND EXPERIENCE 
 Bidder‟s background and experience 
 Bidder‟s expertise and qualifications to com plete this project 
successfully; 
 Bidder‟s experience in the relevant field in cluding products, 
solutions, services and standards. 
 Bidder‟s Key Team Members and Their Qualifications. B. APPROACH AND METHODOLOGY 
 
 The Bidders should demonstrate their understanding of the 
required solution domain;

IIROSA e-Relief RFP 
 
 
 
Confidential Page 10 of 18 
 
 The Bidders should include screen shots of the proposed system 
including some logical diagrams to demonstrate their 
understanding of the workflows and requirements; 
 The Bidders should outline the proposed project management 
methodology they will adopt to complete the task successfully; 
 The Bidder should outline the standards and tools, which they 
will use at the logical and physical design stage. C. PROJECT PLAN AND WORK BREAKDOWN The Bidder must submit a project plan with project work breakd own 
structure and project organization. The project work breakdown 
structure should also identify major deliverables and estimated time to 
complete the deliverables. D. REFERENCES 
A list of at least five references of projects with relevant context must be 
provided, with complete contact information and nature of work 
completed. E. FINANCIAL OFFER 
The financial offer should include detailed breakdown by deliverable. 
 
NOTE: The Financial Offer must be submitted in a separate sealed 
envelope as mentioned in the section 4.3. F. PROPOSAL QUALITY AND PROFESSIONALISM 
Special emphasis will be given to the overall quality and presentation of 
the proposal. 4.2 METHOD OF PROPOSAL SUBMISSION The Bidders must submit one (1) original and two (2) copies of all materials 
(except pricing) required for acceptance of their proposal to: 
 
Public Services Management –Procurements & Contracting department 
International Islamic Relief Organization – Saudi Arabia 
Telephone: 02- 6515411 
Fax: 02 – 6518491 
P.O. Box 14843, Jeddah, KSA 
 
All the pro posals must be submitted latest by Mar 9 , 2011 by 2:00PM KSA 
Time. The receptionist in the above office must receive the proposals. All proposals 
must be packaged, sealed, and must show the RFP Title, Bidder‟s Name and 
Address on the package. Please note, FAX proposals will NOT be accepted 
and any proposal submitted after the due date and time will not be accepted 
or returned back to the Bidder. IIROSA e-Relief RFP 
 
 
 
Confidential Page 11 of 18 
 
4.3 PROPOSAL PRICING Each proposal must submit the pricing only in a separate sealed envelope. The bidders must provide only one cost and it must not reflect anywhere else 
in the prop osal. Any proposal, which does not fulfill this requirement, will 
be disqualified. 4.4 FORMAT OF PROPOSAL 
All documents must be signed by a representative of the bidder's 
organization with authority to contractually bind the organization. Bidders 
responding t o this RFP must comply with the following format 
requirements: 
 
By submitting a signed proposal, the bidder's signatories certify that in 
connection with this procurement: (a) the bidder's organization or an agent 
of the bidder's organization has arrived a t the prices in its proposal without 
consultation, communication or agreement with any other respondent or 
with any competitor for the purpose of restricting competition, (b) the prices 
quoted in the proposal have not been knowingly disclosed by the bidder's 
organization or by any agent of the bidder's organization and will not be 
knowingly disclosed by same, directly or indirectly, to any other respondent 
or to any competitor, and (c) no attempt has been made or will be made by 
the bidder's organization or by any agent of the bidder's organization to 
induce any other person or firm to submit or not to submit a proposal for the 
purpose of restricting competition. 4.5 INCURRING COSTS 
The IIROSA is not liable for any cost incurred by a bidder in the process of 
responding to this RFP. 
4.6 QUESTIONS AND CLARIFICATIONS 
Bidders are expected to raise any questions, exceptions, or additions they 
have concerning the RFP on or before February 27, 2011 to: 
 
IT Management 
International Islamic Relief Organization – Saudi Arabia 
Telephone:

instruction: In RFP for eRelief, quote the section that explains how bidders should handle ambiguities or errors in the RFP document.
input:
DOCUMENT_TAG: RFP for eRelief
SECTION_LEVEL: chapter
SECTION_INDEX: 3
SECTION_TITLE: Communication and Clarifications
SECTION_DESCRIPTION: Contact information and procedures for bidders to request clarifications or report issues with the RFP.
SECTION_TEXT:
02- 6515411 
Fax: 02 – 6518491 
P.O. Box 14843, Jeddah, KSA 
Email: relief@iirosa.org 
 
If a bidder discovers any significant ambiguity, error, conflict, discrepancy, 
omission, or other deficiency in this RFP, the bidder should immediately 
notify IIROSA of such error and request modification or clarification of the 
RFP document. IIROSA e-Relief RFP 
 
 
 
Confidential Page 12 of 18 
 
In the event that it becomes necessary to provide additional clarifying data or 
information, or to revise any part of this RFP, an addendum or revisions will 
be provided to all recipients of this initial RFP. 4.7 NEW RELEASES 
New releases pertaining to the RFP or to the acceptance, rejection, or 
evaluation of proposals shall not be made without the prior written approval 
of the IIROSA. 4.8 CALENDAR OF EVENTS 
Following are the important dates and times related to the release of the RFP. In case, the IIROSA finds it necessary to change any of these dates and times 
it will do so by issuing an addendum to this RFP. NO. EVENT DATE & TIME 
1. Date of Issue of the RFP February 21, 2011 
2. Last Date for Bidder Questions February 12, 2011 
3 Last Date for IIROSA 
Amendment Mar 21, 2011 
4 Proposal Submission Date Mar 09, 2011 by 
2:00PM 
5. Notification of Intent to Award To be Announced 
 
5 PROPOSAL ACCEPTANCE, REVIEW AND AWARD 
5.1 PROPOSAL ACCEPTANCE 
Proposals that do not comply with instructions or are unable to comply with 
mandatory requirements contained in this RFP may be rejected by IIROSA.

instruction: In RFP for eRelief, quote the section that explains the entire Proposal Evaluation and Selection Process including stages and criteria.
input:
DOCUMENT_TAG: RFP for eRelief
SECTION_LEVEL: chapter
SECTION_INDEX: 4
SECTION_TITLE: Proposal Evaluation and Selection Process
SECTION_DESCRIPTION: Information on how proposals will be reviewed, evaluated, and the rights of IIROSA regarding acceptance or rejection.
SECTION_TEXT:
The IIROSA retains the right to accept or reject any or all proposals, or 
accepts or rej ects any part of a proposal deemed to be in the best interest of 
the IIROSA. IIROSA shall be the sole judge as to compliance with the 
instructions contained in this RFP. 5.2 PROPOSAL REVIEW 
Proposals will be reviewed by the IIROSA to verify that they meet all 
specified requirements in this RFP. This verification may include conducting 
demonstrations of the bidder's proposed products(s) and service(s), and 
reviewing results of past awards to the bidder by IIROSA. Bidders may not 
contact IIROSA's reviewer(s) except at the IIROSA's request. IIROSA will 
conduct the evaluation of all the qualified proposals in the four stages: 
5.3 STAGE 1 – MANDATORY REQUIREMENTS 
Any proposal, which does not comply with the mandatory requirements will 
be disqualified and not considered for the next stage. Following is a list of the 
Mandatory requirements:

IIROSA e-Relief RFP 
 
 
 
Confidential Page 13 of 18 
 
 Three (3 ) copies of the proposal, the Original Copy of the 
proposal must indicate as such; 
 A soft copy (CD) of the proposal excluding the price; 
 Proposal Price in a separate sealed envelope without mentioning 
the price anywhere else in the proposal; 
 Completed Checklist Template in the APPENDIX B of this RFP. Following evaluation of the proposals received in response to and in 
compliance with this RFP, IIROSA may, or may not, select bidder(s) with 
whom to attempt to negotiate a contract for products and/or service s. 
Should negotiations fail, IIROSA may or may not choose to negotiate with 
another compliant Bidder. 5.4 STAGE 2 – RATED CRITERIA (100 POINTS) Stage 2 will consist of scoring of each submitted proposal on the basis of the 
Rated Criteria. Following is a list of the rated criteria with score for each 
category: 
 
RATED CRITERIA POINTS 
(1) Background, Qualifications and Experience 10 
(2) Approach and Methodology 45 
(3) Project Plan and Work Breakdown 20 
(4) Managed Services 10 
(5) References 5 
(6) Training 5 
(7) Overall Quality and Proposal Presentation 5 
TOTAL POINTS 100 
 
Following the final scoring based on the rated criteria mentioned above, 
proposals that achieve minimum 75% of score will be deemed qualified for 
consideration under Stage 3 of the process. 
5.5 STAGE 3 – PROPOSAL PRICE At this stage, sealed pricing envelopes will be considered only for those 
proposals that score a minimum of 75% or more. The pricing will be scored using a value based formula. Stage 2 Rated 
Criteria will be divided by the proposal prices as quoted by qualified 
bidders. The lowest value for money and highest value of points for bidder proposal 
will be selected as the winner barring any exceptions or further 
postponements of the project in terms of timelines. IIROSA e-Relief RFP 
 
 
 
Confidential Page 14 of 18 
 
NOTE: IIROSA reserves the right to alter the above mentioned criteria and 
related weights at its own discretion. 5.6 POTENTIAL CHANGE 
IIROSA reserves the right to ask the bidders for a Proof of Concept (POC) 
during the evaluation stage and to go through further price negotiations if 
necessary. 5.7 UNANTICIPATED SERVICES 
IIROSA reserves the right to refuse with the awarded bidder additional fees 
for services unanticipated or not existing at the time of the awarding of the 
contract. 5.8 ACCOUNT REPRESENTATIVE The successful Bidder shall have an Account Representative who shall be the 
primary point of contact between the Bidder and IIROSA. THE FOLLOWING SECTIONS INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED WILL BE 
APPLICABLE AFTER THE CONTRACT IS AWARDED 
6 SECURITY REQUIREMENTS 
6.1 CONFIDENTIALITY 
Contractor acknowledges that some of the data it may become privy to in the 
performance of this Agreement is of a confidential nature and Contractor 
shall make all reasonable efforts to ensure that no such confidential 
information is disseminated or left exposed to unauthorized access by it or its 
employees. Contractor agrees to observe complete confidentiality with respect to al l 
aspects of any confidential information, proprietary data and/or trade 
secrets and any parts thereof, whether such contents are the IIROSA or other 
bidder's whereby Contractor or any Contractor's personnel may gain access 
while engaged by the IIROSA or while on IIROSA premises.

Contractor acknowledges that some of the data it may become privy to in the 
performance of this Agreement is of a confidential nature and Contractor 
shall make all reasonable efforts to ensure that no such confidential 
information is disseminated or left exposed to unauthorized access by it or its 
employees. Contractor agrees to observe complete confidentiality with respect to al l 
aspects of any confidential information, proprietary data and/or trade

instructions contained in this RFP. 5.2 PROPOSAL REVIEW 
Proposals will be reviewed by the IIROSA to verify that they meet all 
specified requirements in this RFP. This verification may include conducting 
demonstrations of the bidder's proposed products(s) and service(s), and 
reviewing results of past awards to the bidder by IIROSA. Bidders may not 
contact IIROSA's reviewer(s) except at the IIROSA's request. IIROSA will

be disqualified and not considered for the next stage. Following is a list of the 
Mandatory requirements:

 Three (3 ) copies of the proposal, the Original Copy of the

Rated Criteria. Following is a list of the rated criteria with score for each 
category: 
 
RATED CRITERIA POINTS

(3) Project Plan and Work Breakdown 20 
(4) Managed Services 10 
(5) References 5

instruction: In RFP for eRelief, quote the section that explains the obligations of the Contractor regarding confidentiality and data security.
input:
DOCUMENT_TAG: RFP for eRelief
SECTION_LEVEL: chapter
SECTION_INDEX: 5
SECTION_TITLE: Intellectual Property and Confidentiality
SECTION_DESCRIPTION: Terms related to intellectual property rights, confidentiality, and security obligations of the contractor.
SECTION_TEXT:
The restrictions 
herein shall survive the termination of this Agreement for any reason and 
shall continue in full force and effect and shall be binding upon the 
Contractor or its agents, employees, successors, assigns, subcontracto rs or 
any party claiming an interest in this Agreement on behalf of or under the 
rights of Contractor following any termination. Contractor shall advise all 
Contractors‟ agents, employees, successors, assigns and subcontractors that 
are engaged by the IIROSA of the restrictions, present and continuing, set 
forth herein. Contractor shall defend and incur all costs, if any, for actions, 
which arise as a result of noncompliance by Contractor, his agents, 
employees, successors, assigns and subcontractors regard ing the restrictions 
herein. The Contractor can not under any circumstances disclose the 
information included in this RFP to any other third party or use it for any 
purpose with other third parties and/or organizations within a time period

IIROSA e-Relief RFP 
 
 
 
Confidential Page 15 of 18 
 
of 5 years from the issuing date of this RFP . In such cases, Contractor will be 
legally liable for such disclosures. 6.2 SECURE STORAGE/TRANSMISSION OF DATA 
All submitted user information and data communication must be encrypted 
using industry standards. 6.3 SECURE PHYSICAL LOCATIONS Any information and/or data obtained by the Contractor by the IIROSA or 
IIROSA's Customers shall be stored in a place physically secure from access 
by unauthorized persons. Contractor shall take every reasonable precaution 
to ensure that all storage areas, and containers ("physical locations") used by 
Contractor in providing the product(s) and service(s) under this Contract 
shall be secure and equipped with reasonable precautions against damage. 7 INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY 
7.1 IIROSA INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY 
The Supplier agrees that all Intellectual Property and every other right, title 
and interest in and to all concepts, techniques, ideas, information and 
materials, however recorded, (including images and data) provided by the 
IIROSA to the Supplier shall re main the sole property of the IIROSA at all 
times. ALL THE BIDDERS MUST NOTE; CONTENT OF THIS RFP INCLUDING 
ALL THE APPENDICES IS THE INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY OF IIROSA. THE BIDDERS MUST NOT USE ANY PART OF THIS RFP FOR ANY 
OTHER PURPOSE OTHER THAN RESPONDING TO THIS RFP WITHOUT 
PRIOR PERMISSION OF IIROSA. 7.2 NO USE OF IIROSA INSIGNIA 
The Supplier shall not use any insignia or logo of the IIROSA except where 
required to provide the Deliverables, and only if it has received the prior 
written permission of the IIROSA to do so. 7.3 OWNERSHIP OF INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY 
The IIROSA shall be the sole owner of any Newly Created Intellectual 
Property. The Supplier irrevocably assigns to and in favor of the IIROSA and 
the IIROSA accepts every right, title and interest in and to all Newly Created 
Intellectual Property in the Deliverables, immediately following the creation 
thereof, for all time. To the extent that any of the Deliverables include, in 
whole or in part, the Supplier‟s Intellectual Property, the Supplier grants to 
the IIROSA a license to use that Supplier Intellectual Property in the manner 
contemplated in this Article, the total consideration for which shall be 
payment of the Rates to the Supplier by the IIROSA. The Supplier shall 
provide the Deliverables to the IIROSA: (a) at the point of completion of its

IIROSA e-Relief RFP 
 
 
 
Confidential Page 16 of 18 
 
obligations under the Agreement; or (b) upon termination of the Agreement; 
or (c) at any time upon the request of the IIROSA. 7.4 PRESUMPTION GOVERNING OWNERSHIP The presumption governing the Agreement shall be that the IIROSA shall be 
the sole owner of any Intellectual Property in any form contained in any of 
the Deliverables. If the Supplier‟s Intellectual Property forms any part of the 
Deliverables, the Supplier shall notify the IIROSA as such prior to the 
delivery of the particular Deliverable containing any such Supplier 
Intellectual Property. In the absence of any such notice the presumption 
shall remain that the IIROSA is the sole owner of any Intellectual Propert y 
contained in the Deliverables. 7.5 NO RESTRICTIVE MATERIAL IN DELIVERABLES The Supplier shall not incorporate into any Deliverables anything that would 
restrict the right of the IIROSA or of any Client to modify, further develop or 
otherwise use the Deliverables in any way that the IIROSA or the Client 
deems necessary, or that would prevent the IIROSA or any Client from 
entering into any Agreement with any Supplier other than the Supplier for 
the modification, further development of or other use of the Deliverables. 7.6 MORAL RIGHTS 
The Supplier shall obtain waiv ers of all rights of integrity and any other 
moral rights in relation to the Deliverables from its employees, volunteers, 
agents and sub -Suppliers and from any other party in the position to assert 
such rights in relation to any of the Deliverables, which waivers may be 
invoked without restriction by any person authorized by the IIROSA to use 
the Deliverables. 7.7 COPYRIGHT NOTICE 
The Supplier shall place a copyright notice on all recorded Deliverables it 
provides to the IIROSA under the Agreement in the following form: 
 
“© IIROSA, [2011]” 
8 LAW, ARBITRATION AND JURISDICTION 
8.1 GOVERNING LAW 
This RFP and all the Agreements related this RFP shall be governed by and 
construed in all respects in accordance with the laws of the Kingdom o f 
Saudi Arabia (KSA). 8.2 JURISDICTION 
All the interested parties must submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the 
courts of Jeddah and the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia (KSA).

invoked without restriction by any person authorized by the IIROSA to use 
the Deliverables. 7.7 COPYRIGHT NOTICE 
The Supplier shall place a copyright notice on all recorded Deliverables it 
provides to the IIROSA under the Agreement in the following form: 
 
“© IIROSA, [2011]” 
8 LAW, ARBITRATION AND JURISDICTION 
8.1 GOVERNING LAW 
This RFP and all the Agreements related this RFP shall be governed by and 
construed in all respects in accordance with the laws of the Kingdom o f 
Saudi Arabia (KSA). 8.2 JURISDICTION 
All the interested parties must submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the 
courts of Jeddah and the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia (KSA).
output: The Supplier must keep all RFP information confidential for five years and ensure secure handling of data. All intellectual property provided by IIROSA or created during the contract belongs exclusively to IIROSA. The Supplier cannot use IIROSA's logos without permission, must waive moral rights on deliverables, and must not include restrictive materials that limit IIROSA's future use. The Supplier must deliver all work upon completion or termination and comply with Saudi Arabian law and jurisdiction.

For

For

eRelief is initially intended to serve as a unified platform for the employees 
of IIROSA around the globe , to input and retrieve real -time relief 
information. The purpose of this RFP is to select a qualified Vendor who can deliv er a 
comprehensive eRelief Platform featuring:

Confidential Page 14 of 18

instruction: In RFP for eRelief, quote the section that explains the evaluation and selection process for proposals.
input:
DOCUMENT_TAG: RFP for eRelief
SECTION_LEVEL: topic
SECTION_INDEX: 2
SECTION_TITLE: Evaluation and Selection Process
SECTION_DESCRIPTION: Explains how IIROSA will evaluate submitted proposals, including criteria, potential changes, negotiation rights, and the possibility of proof of concept requests.
SECTION_TEXT:
The IIROSA retains the right to accept or reject any or all proposals, or 
accepts or rej ects any part of a proposal deemed to be in the best interest of 
the IIROSA. Following evaluation of the proposals received in response to and in 
compliance with this RFP, IIROSA may, or may not, select bidder(s) with 
whom to attempt to negotiate a contract for products and/or service s. 
Should negotiations fail, IIROSA may or may not choose to negotiate with 
another compliant Bidder. IIROSA will 
conduct the evaluation of all the qualified proposals in the four stages: 
5.3 STAGE 1 – MANDATORY REQUIREMENTS 
Any proposal, which does not comply with the mandatory requirements will 
be disqualified and not considered for the next stage. This verification may include conducting 
demonstrations of the bidder's proposed products(s) and service(s), and 
reviewing results of past awards to the bidder by IIROSA. 5.2 PROPOSAL REVIEW 
Proposals will be reviewed by the IIROSA to verify that they meet all 
specified requirements in this RFP.

instruction: In RFP for eRelief, quote the section that explains how access control to services is managed and the interface used.

SECTION_TEXT:
Access control to the 
different services will be role-based depending on the user‟s 
department. The resulting relief services should be accessible from a unified 
web interface, through the IIROSA intranet. The platform will take care of the complexity of interacting with 
all the relevant back -end systems that are in place, providing an integrated 
single interface serving up IIROSA relief services. The relief services layer should cover all of the processes and 
services provided under the following welfare and relief domains: 
1. Social Welfare Program

Architecture style.

instruction: In RFP for eRelief, quote the section that explains the ownership of intellectual property created during the project.
input:

SECTION_LEVEL: topic
SECTION_INDEX: 7
SECTION_TITLE: Intellectual Property and Licensing
SECTION_DESCRIPTION: Outlines the ownership, licensing rights, and restrictions related to intellectual property included in the deliverables.
SECTION_TEXT:
7.3 OWNERSHIP OF INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY 
The IIROSA shall be the sole owner of any Newly Created Intellectual 
Property. 7 INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY 
7.1 IIROSA INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY 
The Supplier agrees that all Intellectual Property and every other right, title 
and interest in and to all concepts, techniques, ideas, information and 
materials, however recorded, (including images and data) provided by the 
IIROSA to the Supplier shall re main the sole property of the IIROSA at all 
times. 15 
7 INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY ...................................................................... 15 
7.1 IIROSA INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY ................................................................. 15 
7.2 NO USE OF IIROSA INSIGNIA ............................................................................ 15 
7.3 OWNERSHIP OF INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY ............................................... 15 
7.4 PRESUMPTION GOVERNING OWNERSHIP ................................................... The presumption governing the Agreement shall be that the IIROSA shall be 
the sole owner of any Intellectual Property in any form contained in any of 
the Deliverables. In the absence of any such notice the presumption 
shall remain that the IIROSA is the sole owner of any Intellectual Propert y

instruction: In RFP for eRelief, quote the section that explains the confidentiality obligations of the contractor after the contract is awarded.
input:
DOCUMENT_TAG: RFP for eRelief
SECTION_LEVEL: topic
SECTION_INDEX: 8
SECTION_TITLE: Confidentiality and Security Obligations
SECTION_DESCRIPTION: Details the contractor's responsibilities to protect data, storage areas, and maintain confidentiality throughout and after the contract period.
SECTION_TEXT:
THE FOLLOWING SECTIONS INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED WILL BE 
APPLICABLE AFTER THE CONTRACT IS AWARDED 
6 SECURITY REQUIREMENTS 
6.1 CONFIDENTIALITY 
Contractor acknowledges that some of the data it may become privy to in the 
performance of this Agreement is of a confidential nature and Contractor 
shall make all reasonable efforts to ensure that no such confidential 
information is disseminated or left exposed to unauthorized access by it or its 
employees. Contractor agrees to observe complete confidentiality with respect to al l 
aspects of any confidential information, proprietary data and/or trade 
secrets and any parts thereof, whether such contents are the IIROSA or other 
bidder's whereby Contractor or any Contractor's personnel may gain access 
while engaged by the IIROSA or while on IIROSA premises. In such cases, Contractor will be 
legally liable for such disclosures. The Contractor can not under any circumstances disclose the 
information included in this RFP to any other third party or use it for any 
purpose with other third parties and/or organizations within a time period

IIROSA e-Relief RFP 
 
 
 
Confidential Page 15 of 18 
 
of 5 years from the issuing date of this RFP . Contractor shall take every reasonable precaution 
to ensure that all storage areas, and containers ("physical locations") used by 
Contractor in providing the product(s) and service(s) under this Contract 
shall be secure and equipped with reasonable precautions against damage.
